key_insight: Cepstral analysis makes Green-Kubo thermal conductivity calculations for MOFs far more stable, reproducible, and faster to converge than conventional direct correlation analysis.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical, near-ab initio prediction of heat transport in porous materials, supporting better design of MOF-based devices where thermal management matters.
method: Pair equilibrium molecular dynamics Green-Kubo simulations with cepstral analysis.
Use machine-learned moment tensor potentials trained on DFT reference data.
Test the approach on MOF-5, HKUST-1, and ZIF-8.
evidence_signal: Across three representative MOFs, the cepstral approach gave stable thermal conductivity estimates over a wide range of correlation lengths and converged in about 1–2 ns of total sampling time.
watch_item: Results still depend on the quality and transferability of the trained interatomic potential, so reliable DFT training data and careful model validation remain important.
paper_title: Cepstral Analysis to accelerate Green-Kubo thermal conductivity calculations of Metal-Organic Frameworks
